Name: GitHub - Push Yeoman Generator
Description: Clones a GitHub repo, runs Yeoman in the cloned directory, and pushes the changes. Note that the Yeoman generators can only use arguments or options, as prompts can not be provided.

Parameters.Name: PopulateGithubRepo.Yeoman.Generator.Package
Parameters.Label: Yeoman Generator Package
Parameters.HelpText: The package containing the Yeoman package. Yeoman packages are usually distributed by npm, but they can also be packaged as zip files and pushed to the built-in feed or any other feed that supports zip files.

Parameters.Name: PopulateGithubRepo.Yeoman.Generator.Name
Parameters.Label: Yeoman Generator Name
Parameters.HelpText: The name of the Yeoman generator.

Parameters.Name: PopulateGithubRepo.Yeoman.Generator.SubGenerator
Parameters.Label: Yeoman Subgenerator Name
Parameters.HelpText: The optional name of the Yeoman subgenerator. Leave blank to use the default generator.

Parameters.Name: PopulateGithubRepo.Yeoman.Generator.Arguments
Parameters.Label: Yeoman Arguments
Parameters.HelpText: The optional arguments pass to Yeoman to define options and arguments.

Parameters.Name: PopulateGithubRepo.Git.Url.Repo
Parameters.Label: GitHub Repo Name
Parameters.HelpText: The GitHub repo name i.e. `myrepo` in the URL`https://github.com/owner/myrepo`.

Parameters.Name: PopulateGithubRepo.Git.Url.Organization
Parameters.Label: Github Owner
Parameters.HelpText: The GitHub repo owner or organization i.e. `owner` in the URL `https://github.com/owner/myrepo`.

Parameters.Name: PopulateGithubRepo.Git.Credentials.Username
Parameters.Label: GitHub Username
Parameters.HelpText: This will appear in the commit logs. Leave blank to use the default username.
